type: Vertical B2B/prosumer micro-SaaS — financial + CPD dashboard for UK associate dentists
one_line_reason: Cheap ($350, 1.6x) and Stripe-verified, with a real founder-partnership path — but as a solo asset-buy the value transfers away with the dentist (domain expertise + dental-community distribution), so value_transfers=false caps it at MARGINAL (provisional); worth pursuing only if the founder commits to partnering.

operability_upside.note: Simple web app + Stripe, squarely in the team's stack — trivially runnable with modest product upside. (The distribution/domain gap now lives in the growth-lever gate + Transfer, not double-counted here.)

pmf_retention.note: Stripe-verified but tiny: $30 MRR, 2 subs after ~6 months, 'positive feedback, no real marketing.' PMF essentially unproven.

unit_economics.note: Good MODEL (B2B recurring, GBP14/mo, professionals who pay for tax/time savings) but negligible current revenue; LTV:CoCA unproven.

moat_defensibility.note: DR 0, LinkedIn-only, no moat yet. A potential moat (dental-community trust + workflow depth) exists in principle but is entirely unbuilt.

growth_market.note: Real, stable single-country vertical (UK associate dentists); category isn't dying, but each expansion (other countries/professions) needs fresh domain work.

tech_ip.note: Simple web app + Stripe, UK GDPR compliant, presumably transferable; full ownership unconfirmed. Holding dentists' financial data adds a compliance/security burden.

transfer_key_person.note: Much of the demand rides the dentist-founder's domain expertise and dental-community relationships — value_transfers=false. A committed partnership is the only thing that would carry it across.

deal_terms.note: $350 (1.6x revenue) is essentially free and low-risk on cash; founder offers onboarding + 'consider partnership' (buyer-friendly). Only ding: cheap because there's ~nothing there yet, and 8 competing offers.

seller_adverse_selection.note: Transparent, identifiable real UK dentist, Stripe-verified, honest about tiny traction. Low deception risk; mild adverse selection — a nice idea that didn't take off.

key_unknowns: Will the dentist-founder actually partner (domain + dental-community distribution) — the single factor that flips the growth lever from a team weakness to viable. Without him you're engineers guessing at UK dental finance with no channel.
Real usage beyond the 2 paid subs: free/active users, trial->paid conversion, and what the 'positive feedback' concretely asked for.
Is there any repeatable channel into UK associate dentists (BDA, CPD providers, dental forums), or was it all the founder's personal network? (drives growth_lever_fits)
How much UK-specific domain logic is baked in (CPD hours, HMRC/tax, associate pay), and the compliance load of holding dentists' financial data.
Full IP/data ownership and transferability; stack/hosting (drives ip_transferable).
seller_questions: Would you stay on as a domain + distribution PARTNER (rev-share/equity) — we're strong engineers but not dentists or UK dental marketers? On what terms? (decisive: value_transfers and growth_lever hinge on this)
Beyond the 2 paying subs: how many free/active users, what's trial->paid, and what specifically did the 'positive feedback' ask you to build? (PMF)
How did the first users find DentFlow, and what channels realistically reach UK associate dentists (associations, CPD providers, dental forums/FB)? (growth lever)
What UK-specific logic is built in (CPD rules, tax/HMRC, associate pay structures), and what compliance obligations come with holding dentists' financial data (GDPR/data processing)? (domain + tech)
Is 100% of code, domain, and user data owned by you and transferable? Any contractors? What's the stack and hosting? (IP/transfer)
Why sell at $350 after ~6 months, and what are the 8 offers/other interested buyers? (adverse selection + competition)

scorecard_markdown:
## Acquisition Scorecard (v2) — DentFlow

**Verdict: MARGINAL (provisional) — merit 48/100, completeness 66%**

Cheap ($350, 1.6x revenue) and Stripe-verified, with a genuine founder-partnership path — but the value walks away with the dentist-founder (domain expertise + dental-community distribution), so `value_transfers=false` caps it at MARGINAL. Provisional because the growth lever and IP transfer are still unknown. Affordable ($350 well under the $3,000 ceiling); revenue is buyer-verified (Stripe via TrustMRR).

### Dimensions (score/5, confidence)
- Operability & upside: 3 (med) — simple web app + Stripe, in-stack, trivially runnable; modest product upside.
- PMF & retention: 1 (high) — $30 MRR, 2 subs after ~6 months; PMF essentially unproven.
- Unit economics: 2 (med) — good recurring model (GBP14/mo), but negligible revenue; LTV:CoCA unproven.
- Moat & defensibility: 2 (med) — DR 0, LinkedIn-only, no moat yet; potential dental-community moat is unbuilt.
- Growth & market: 3 (med) — real, stable UK dental vertical; expansion needs fresh domain work each time.
- Tech & IP health: 3 (med) — simple, GDPR-compliant, presumably transferable; ownership unconfirmed; data-compliance burden.
- Transfer / key-person: 2 (med) — demand rides the founder's domain + relationships; value_transfers=false.
- Deal terms & value: 4 (med) — $350 (1.6x) essentially free, buyer-friendly, partnership on offer; 8 competing offers.
- Seller & adverse-selection: 3 (med) — transparent real UK dentist, Stripe-verified, honest; mild adverse selection.

### Gates
- operable: TRUE
- growth_lever_fits: UNKNOWN (no established channel; "no real marketing done yet") -> provisional
- ip_transferable: UNKNOWN -> provisional
- value_transfers: FALSE -> caps at MARGINAL (dentist domain/community walks with founder)
- not_fragile: TRUE (no structural single point of failure; early thinness graded in Moat/Transfer)
- revenue_evidence: BUYER_VERIFIED (Stripe)

### Key unknowns
- Founder partnership commitment (decisive for growth lever + value transfer).
- Real usage beyond 2 paid subs; trial->paid.
- Repeatable channel into UK associate dentists vs. founder's personal network.
- Depth of UK-specific domain logic + data-compliance load.
- Full IP/data ownership and transferability.
